title: Nail Design Inspired by the Catalan Music Museum
personalObjective: To develop my manicure skills by applying new and creative techniques, drawing inspiration from the art of the Palau de la Música Catalana to create unique designs that combine art and technique.
personalChallenge: I was afraid of not achieving a good result, as many techniques were completely unknown to me, especially using foils and chrome effects. I found it difficult to shape nails anatomically correctly and apply gel properly.

investigation.title: Investigation
investigation.content:
I drew inspiration from the art of the Palau de la Música Catalana (Catalan Music Palace), as I visited this museum where they explained very interesting aspects about music, the history of instruments, and various works. It was a very enriching experience for me.

Recently, in September, I was gifted a lamp and other materials to start doing nails. I enjoyed it quite a bit, so I began practicing more by doing my nails and my mother's, trying different designs. I bought creative art materials on Shein to practice more designs and improve overall.

As I'm just starting out, I still find it quite difficult to shape nails anatomically correctly, apply gel properly, use a magnet for cat eye gel, etc.

preparation.title: Preparation and Planning
preparation.content:
So I started researching how I could achieve this angel effect, something more luminous. I have a white cat eye gel that I could use. I also have some angel foils, but they're quite difficult to use without the designs breaking.

Additionally, I've been learning how to apply the chrome effect, as I have a gold chrome that could work for me. I planned to first practice on test nails before applying the techniques to my own nails to ensure I did it correctly.

action.title: Action
action.content:
I started with the manicure itself: cutting the cuticle and preparing the nails. Then I applied the base and shaped the nails with a more square form, as many didn't have side walls and I had to reconstruct them.

First, I did the middle finger nail. I applied the cat eye, but encountered the problem that it looked too gray, so I decided to apply the chrome afterward. I did one of the nails on the other hand the same way.

The next design was even more complicated. It was my first time using foils, which are basically designs that transfer to the nail, but it's actually more complex than it seems. To make sure I did it right, I first practiced on a test nail. I applied the foil glue and waited a minute until it became transparent. Then I placed the foil with the shiny side up. I pressed gently so it would adhere correctly. When I removed it, it left a beautiful design that clearly reminded me of the museum's motifs.

On other nails I decided to decorate, I had to draw the motifs by hand, inspired by the reference images I had.

demonstration.title: Demonstration
demonstration.content:
After drying the drawings with the lamp, I applied the gold chrome. Finally, I applied the top coat, dried it, and obtained the final result.

The final design combines several techniques: white cat eye with gold chrome on some nails, foils with angel motifs on others, and hand-drawn designs inspired by the ornaments of the Palau de la Música Catalana. The gold and white tones recreate the elegance and luminosity of the museum's art.

reflection.title: Reflection
reflection.content:
This activity has been very interesting and I feel I've learned a lot. It was challenging to think about how to apply, at least, the techniques I have access to in order to obtain the result I wanted, while learning new methods.

At first I was enthusiastic about such a creative idea, but when it came to carrying it out and planning it, I felt quite a bit of pressure because I was afraid it wouldn't turn out well, as many techniques were unknown to me. I made several mistakes, but that's part of the learning process, and next time it will turn out better.

Overall, manicure has become an activity I'm passionate about and the field in which I want to continue developing my skills. It's very gratifying to be able to do my own nails, and that they're not just nails, but a form of art; every time I look at them I can feel proud of what I've achieved.
